titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Norman Walsh Taylor papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Rachel Mihalko

publicationstmt.publisher: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS).

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday May 2, 2023 at 23:00

corpname.part: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

did.abstract: The collection contains material created by Norman Walsh Taylor related to his time at Yale Drama School, Union Theological Seminary, and other playwriting endeavors. It includes theatrical production photographs, scripts, class notes, and some biographical memorabilia.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in two series: I. Playwriting and II. Personal papers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Norman Walsh Taylor papers, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Norman Walsh Taylor papers document Taylor's development and career as a playwright form his time at Yale Drama School and later at Union Theological Seminary. The papers are composed chiefly notes, sketches, and drafts of his plays, and photographs and programs relating to production. Additional materials are also present, including college notes, childhood memorabilia, and photographs and floorplans related to Davenport Theatre.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Norman Walsh Taylor was a graduate of Carleton and Yale Drama School (1956). Born Norman Walsh, Taylor used a mononym, "Taylor" for several years before changing his name to Norman Walsh Taylor. He studied religious drama at Union Theological Seminary in New York, and later became a professor of English literature at State University in New York City. He wrote articles, won a Canadian playwriting contest, and his expedition down the Amazon River led to a documentary about mercury pollution in Grassy Narrows, Ontario. He became a Quaker and developed courses in peace and nonviolence, working on a project in Mississippi addressing violence against black voters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files include rough notes, drafts, and character studies.

Douglas Thomas: a three act play

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This script was created for a TV-Radio Workshop of the Ford Foundation, Omnibus V, Volume 5.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Documents were removed from metal clasp binder for preservation purposes.

scopecontent.p: This performance occurred as it won 2nd place in the Canadian Playwriting Competition

The Relationship of the Playwright to his Theatre

scopecontent.p: Second part to this essay is the script for Inside

did.unittitle: Off-Broadway: Cafe Drama and the Witch Hunt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of files relating to Norman Walsh Taylor's studies in playwriting as well as his teaching.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Published works were removed from the collection, including several plays by Thornton Wilder and compilations of short plays by Margaret Mayorga. Some plays were used in Taylor's teaching exercises, including Wilder's "The Skin of Our Teeth". Other Wilder plays removed include The Ides of March, The Angel that Troubled the Waters, The Long Christmas Dinner, The Woman of Andros, Love and How to Cure It, Queens of France, and The Happy Journey. Please see Orbis catalogue for copies available at Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspaper sections

did.unittitle: Carleton student newspaper from Carleton College, Ottawa, bound copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Available through the Internet Archive

arrangement.p: Paper removed from black three ring binder for preservation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain Norman Walsh Taylor's annotated copies of the plays.

scopecontent.p: Includes articles, instruction, and student papers

did.unittitle: Portraits of Norman Walsh Taylor, taken by Dorothy Kehaya

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File contains a class project that includes an analysis of the play.

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings and photographs from ventriloquist performances

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Norman Walsh met Butler Davenport in 1957 and refurbished his theatre and mounted two productions into repertory. Butler Davenport entrusted Norman Walsh with material that is now included in this collection, including copies of Davenport's scripts written under pseudonyms, photographs and floorplans of the building at its original opening, and printed material related to the theatre's history.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File includes images of building interior and exterior, advertising, set design, and stills from The Servant of Two Masters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File includes photographs and preparatory research for production of Carlo Godoni's play.

did.unittitle: Noye's Fluddle by Benjamin Britten at St. George's Episcopal Church, performance program and photographs
